cases.title: M01 protocol epoch replaces the scaffold before production consumers
expected: Canonical subsystem packages replace the non-production dennett.v1 scaffold as one explicit epoch
Handshake, authenticated bridge session, bootstrap, project, session, cancel and explicit result-or-error contracts generate for Rust and TypeScript
System and session watches start with snapshots, carry Authority Epoch in every cursor and expose monotonic deltas plus resync
Create admission allocates stable identity without claiming completion; System watch publishes a correlated terminal result and authoritative state mutation
Descriptor checks pin exact services, streaming modes, field numbers, presence and oneof membership
Buf STANDARD lint passes with no grandfathered file exceptions and negative lint probes still fail
The checker-owned compatibility baseline changes only through reviewed epoch evidence and still rejects field-number reuse
DEBT-0001 and DEBT-0002 are retired only after generated clients and migration checks pass

cases.title: Codex SDK adapter preserves the provider boundary
expected: Deterministic tests exercise the adapter without a live model call
Provider-specific SDK event types do not escape the Node adapter host
The canary pins SDK and CLI 0.144.6, the built-in OpenAI provider and exact ChatGPT login status while removing API-key and custom-provider environment variables
Tool, file-change, MCP and web-search stream items fail the canary instead of being accepted as connectivity evidence
Malformed event ordering, identities, item fields, usage and late iterator cleanup fail closed with normalized diagnostics
Logs and fixtures contain no authentication token, prompt text or private response content
The official SDK version is pinned by the workspace lockfile

cases.title: Codex SDK live canary uses ChatGPT subscription authentication
expected: The login and canary reject API-key mode and confirm a dedicated Codex-managed ChatGPT subscription session without reading token values or touching normal Codex authentication
The live turns run in a deadline-bounded disposable Git workspace with isolated home and Git configuration that contains no project files and is removed afterward
A temporary isolated CODEX_HOME exposes only a same-volume filesystem link to the dedicated canary authentication and does not load user configuration, plugins, skills or MCP servers
One trivial streamed turn reaches a terminal success through the official Codex SDK
A second turn emits the same observed SDK thread identity and both turns have bounded deadlines
The report contains versions, event kinds and latency but no prompt, response, token or credential values

cases.title: Project Chat renders the approved workbench states
expected: The default screen contains a narrow Activity Rail, session/project sidebar, direct chat, compact composer toolbar and collapsible read-only auxiliary pane
User messages use restrained gray surfaces while agent messages remain unboxed
Loading, streaming, stopped, timed-out, stale, resyncing, empty and restored fixtures are reviewable
No working Git, diff approval or project mutation action is implied before M02

cases.title: Project Chat remains operable by keyboard and assistive technology
expected: All primary controls are keyboard reachable with visible focus and no keyboard trap
Stop is discoverable and scoped to the active session
Streaming status is announced in bounded updates rather than once per token
Text, controls and focus indicators meet the selected contrast and target-size baseline

cases.title: Agent runtimes emit ordered provider-neutral stream events
expected: Every stream starts, advances monotonically and reaches at most one terminal outcome
Text, progress, usage and completion events are normalized independently of the provider SDK
Bounded versioned provider-native activity metadata survives the adapter, Head journal and desktop boundary without becoming a core behavior dependency
Late or malformed events are rejected without inventing completion
The scripted fake and Codex adapter execute the same conformance cases

cases.title: Cancellation is scoped acknowledged and terminal
expected: A cancel command identifies the exact session and active runtime turn
Cancellation is idempotent and emits an authoritative acknowledgement
A cancelled turn cannot later be reported as completed
Cancelling one turn does not cancel another session

cases.title: Runtime deadlines produce an honest timeout outcome
expected: The scripted runtime can deterministically exceed a configured deadline
Timeout is distinct from cancellation, refusal and transport failure
Partial output remains explicitly partial
A late provider completion cannot overwrite the timeout outcome

cases.title: Codex continuation remains opaque and resumable
expected: Core receives an opaque continuation reference rather than a Codex SDK object
The adapter resumes a prior Codex thread through the pinned SDK
Missing provider continuation state returns a recoverable error
Provider replacement does not require changing project-session identity

cases.title: Project session restores authoritative turns after restart
expected: Accepted user turns and terminal runtime outcomes are durably committed before acknowledgement
Restart reconstructs the same project and session identity in order
Completed, partial, cancelled and timed-out turns retain distinct states
Integrity or migration failure stops restore with an actionable error instead of silently dropping history

cases.title: A watch sequence gap marks state stale and requires resync
expected: A snapshot establishes stream identity and base revision
Duplicate deltas are no-ops and a future sequence gap enters resyncing
No later delta is applied until a fresh snapshot is accepted
Unavailable state preserves the last permitted value while protected content is removed on revoke

cases.title: Unsent desktop draft survives UI restart without becoming canonical memory
expected: An unsent composer draft restores after UI restart
The draft is visibly unsent and never appears as a canonical session turn
Sending the restored draft creates one command with a stable command identifier
Discarding the draft does not mutate authoritative project history

implementation.target_suite: desktop bridge and Node draft recovery

cases.title: Local draft cache restores without entering canonical session history
expected: A draft record and its stable command identifier restore after the control store reopens
Draft edits retain one command identifier and a conflicting replacement is rejected
Draft rows never appear in the canonical project-session event journal
Sending once is idempotent and discarding the draft does not mutate canonical history

implementation.target_suite: draft cache port and embedded Head recovery

cases.title: Desktop and Node establish a versioned authenticated local IPC session
expected: Windows uses a per-install Named Pipe with OS-local access controls and no default TCP listener
The Named Pipe DACL is protected and semantically grants full access only to the current Windows user
ClientHello and ServerWelcome negotiate a supported protocol version and short-lived session proof
Each accepted connection permits one bounded handshake attempt; expired or disconnected sessions and replay challenges cannot grow without limit
Unauthenticated same-user clients are limited per process and evicted by a pre-authentication deadline without terminating the accept loop
Connect, handshake, bootstrap and watch-open operations have bounded deadlines with retryable transport-neutral errors
Invalid challenge, incompatible protocol and wrong installation identity are rejected
The React renderer never receives Node credentials or raw endpoint secrets

cases.title: Closing the desktop window leaves Node and active state alive
expected: Desktop startup renders a local shell before bootstrap completes and reports freshness
Closing or crashing the Tauri window does not terminate dennett-node
Abrupt termination of a real desktop-launcher process leaves the detached Node reachable by successive authenticated clients
Reopening creates a new UI session, reconnects and restores the prior authoritative session
Node absent and protocol mismatch lead to Recovery actions instead of an empty application

cases.title: Tauri forwards typed watch frames without owning domain state
expected: Tauri validates command payloads and forwards stable command identifiers
The command response is the only delivery path for the initial snapshot; the channel then carries ordered delta and recovery frames without reimplementing session semantics
Concurrent authoritative commits are published in revision order and delta or revision-gap recovery fields survive DTO conversion
Closing a watch promptly cancels the complete in-flight reconnect pipeline, including a hung Node start
Reconnect and Node-absent errors map to transport-neutral client errors
Frontend features depend on DennettClient rather than importing Tauri APIs directly

cases.title: Local desktop conversation completes and restores end to end
expected: A user creates a project session and receives an ordered streamed fake response in Project Chat
Concise provider commentary and effect activity remain in provider order, low-level reasoning is not exposed, and only the final completed agent message becomes the answer
Provider activity and the final answer are presented separately with readable spacing, elapsed work time, rendered Markdown and fixed message clock times
Runtime controls use compact application-styled menus and show only the active adapter and choices that the provider actually advertises; unavailable choices and resource placeholders are absent
Access, model, reasoning and speed selections cross the renderer, Tauri, authenticated IPC, Head and adapter boundary and configure the submitted Codex turn
Codex Auto-approve and Full access choices map to enforced non-interactive sandbox settings rather than presentation-only labels
A standalone Recent chat is durably listed with no project identity and runs in the Node-owned scratch workspace
An in-flight clarification is durably recorded, fenced to the exact active agent turn and delivered through native Codex turn/steer without cancellation or a replacement turn
Provider commentary and effects remain on the correct side of an in-flight clarification, and Stop terminalizes any unfinished activity indicator
The terminal Result Envelope and matching Memory Event share the submitted command identity
Stop reaches an authoritative cancelled state and is not lost
After the desktop window closes and reopens, Node remains alive and the same session is restored
The same application path can select the live Codex adapter without changing UI or core contracts
Automated tests qualify each renderer, Tauri, IPC, Node, Head and runtime boundary; the final joined native-window path is accepted through the required live owner demo

implementation.target_suite: layered deterministic qualification plus native owner demo and opt-in Codex canary

cases.title: Provider timeout is visible recoverable and never shown as success
expected: A deterministic fake timeout produces a timed-out authoritative turn
Project Chat shows the timeout, retained partial output and an explicit retry path
The UI never derives success from model text or optimistic state
A late provider event cannot replace the visible terminal timeout

implementation.target_suite: Head deadline and native Project Chat recovery

cases.title: One privacy-aware trace joins the complete M01 turn
expected: Trace evidence joins project, session, command, runtime turn, provider and memory event identifiers
Component and protocol-version attributes identify each boundary
Prompt text, response text, credentials and private object identifiers are absent from metric labels
A failed or cancelled turn remains diagnosable through the same correlation chain
